entry.title: Re: Reschedule exam (new format) to an earlier date
entry.content:
Hi @mariagiovanna, you can definitely move your exam to an earlier date! Just go to your "My Learning" dashboard on the SAP Learning Hub and select "Reschedule".

Just a reminder: make sure to finalize the change at least 24 hours before your original slot to avoid losing the attempt. If you're looking for an earlier date that isn't currently showing, try checking the portal a few times a day—slots often open up when others reschedule. Good luck with your C_ADBTP certification!

entry.title: LIST_TO_ASCI no longer works with ABAPLIST in EHP7
entry.content:
Hi everyone.
I have the following function in a program that worked fine in R3 and ECC6, but now that I'm trying it in EHP7, it gives me an error.
 
  DATA  BEGIN OF v_asci OCCURS 0.
          INCLUDE STRUCTURE abaplist.
  DATA  END OF v_asci.
  
  DATA: mem_tab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF abaplist.
 
[...]
  
    CALL FUNCTION 'LIST_FROM_MEMORY'
      TABLES
        listobject = mem_tab
      EXCEPTIONS
        not_found = 1
        others = 2.
    IF not mem_tab[] is initial.
      REFRESH v_asci.
      CALL FUNCTION 'LIST_TO_ASCI'
*        EXPORTING
*          CONVERT_SYMB_AND_ICON_2_TEXT = abap_true
        TABLES
          listasci   = v_asci
          listobject = mem_tab
        EXCEPTIONS
          empty_list = 1
          list_index_invalid = 2
          others = 3.
  
[...]
  
 
The error it gives me is that "listasci" must be a pure text table.
I saw that compared to the previous release, there's an extra parameter: CONVERT_SYMB_AND_ICON_2_TEXT. I tried setting it in abap_true, but it doesn't change anything; it gives me the same error.
 
Do I have to create a text-only table?
I tried that too, but then I can't get an abaplist table like before.
 
What can I do?
I'd like to change as little code as possible.

entry.title: Re: Updating Stock Type / System Status in Material Seria...
entry.content:
Hi @edoymaner, you've hit a standard system constraint. Fields like Stock Type and System Status are strictly transaction-driven to maintain a Clean Core.

You cannot PATCH them directly because they must stay in sync with the physical inventory. Instead of the Serial Number API, use the API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT to post a transfer or movement. When you include the serial number in the material document payload, SAP will update those fields for you automatically. For long-term governance, I'd recommend tracking these status transitions in SAP Cloud ALM and validating the flow with Tosca API Testing to ensure no "orphan" serial numbers are created during high-volume movements.

entry.title: Re: Poland KSeF eDocument Cockpit - "The data object coul...
entry.content:
Hi @JoseGuill, I completely understand your frustration—this is a high-stakes "Clean Core" issue where the platform's internal mapping appears to be broken in your Production tenant.

The double space in the error usually means the variable for the Type Name is initial (null) at runtime. This often happens if the AIF Metadata or the eDocument Proxy wasn't fully activated during the production cutover.

Have you verified if there is a difference in the Feature Toggle status between the two systems? Sometimes a specific KSeF "Early Adopter" toggle is active in Test but was missed in Production. I’d also recommend using SAP Cloud ALM to document this discrepancy and escalate it as a "System Inconsistency" rather than a configuration error to get it routed to the development team faster.

entry.title: Re: Bug regression regarding WSL in SAP CDS Language Supp...
entry.content:
Hi @JohannesZinnau, this definitely looks like a regression where the extension is losing its "WSL-awareness" during the 9.9 update. The error confirms that the cds-typer is being invoked in a Windows context while looking for a Linux environment string.

Aside from downgrading, try running cds build --for typescript manually in your WSL terminal to keep your types updated. I’d also recommend reporting this on the CAP GitHub or through a support ticket, as this breaks the "Clean Core" local development experience. You can use SAP Cloud ALM to track these extension bugs within your team's development environment to ensure everyone stays on the stable 9.8 version until a fix is released.

entry.title: Re: Is this a sign SAP is losing the AI race?
entry.content:
This is a powerful analysis, @joao_sousa2. You've identified the core challenge: the difference between technical governance and market protectionism.

If Joule is to succeed, it must become an "Expert Guide" that adds value general LLMs can't touch, such as native understanding of complex SAP financial and logistics structures. Mandating Joule via A2A protocols might solve the stability issue, but it risks alienating developers who are already seeing massive productivity gains from "open" AI tools like Claude.

Moving forward, the most successful implementations will likely be those that use SAP Cloud ALM to govern these new "integration seams" while pushing SAP to make Joule more capable through deep-tier skills and better support for local/hybrid AI models.

entry.title: Re: Building Custom Joule Capabilities: Role-Based Scenario Access in Joule
entry.content:
This is a masterclass in implementing "Clean Core" security principles for AI extensions, @david_bizer!

Using the visibility_condition field to gate scenarios based on IAS identity tokens is far superior to trying to handle permissions within the function logic or via the LLM prompt. It prevents "intent leaking" where a user might discover the existence of a restricted scenario simply by asking the right questions.

A few key takeaways for those implementing this:

  • Implicit AND: Remember that adding multiple objects to the visibility_condition array creates an implicit AND condition—excellent for cross-validating group membership with specific Work Zone navigation targets.

  • Attribute Versatility: Beyond just groups, the support for user_uuid and email is a lifesaver for designated testing phases or managing high-privilege capability owners.

  • Session Latency: As noted, since these attributes are baked into the JWT at login, users might need to re-authenticate after an IAS group change to see the new scenarios in Joule.

This declarative approach keeps the "integration seams" between Joule and IAS robust and manageable. Looking forward to the next part on Document Grounding!

entry.title: Re: Billing Split issue solution order management
entry.content:
This is a common challenge with the #4GT scope item, @alexbeer! The split occurs because the "integration seams" between the Service BDR and the Sales Delivery don't share identical header fields like Customer Reference or Assignment by default.

To resolve this without breaking your Clean Core, you should indeed use the Cloud BAdI SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER. This allows you to programmatically align the header fields (like VBRK-BSTNK) so the system perceives them as a single logical transaction.

For long-term governance, I recommend using SAP Cloud ALM to document this custom logic and validating it with Tricentis Tosca to ensure that future S/4HANA upgrades don't revert your header alignment and re-introduce the splits.

entry.title: Re: Esri ArcGIS license requirement for SAP Spatial asset...
entry.content:
Hi @shirsendu_roy2, you are on the right track. An Esri ArcGIS license is not mandatory for SAP Spatial Asset Management.

If your requirements are met by OpenStreetMap and the Spatial Data Explorer, you can run a "GIS-agnostic" architecture where SAP HANA acts as the primary spatial database. You only need ArcGIS integration (and services like Sync4GEF) if your business requires specialized GIS functionalities like advanced topological editing or network trace analysis that OpenStreetMap cannot provide.

To maintain a Clean Core, I recommend sticking with your current OSM approach and using SAP Cloud ALM to govern the spatial data structures in HANA. You can also validate your spatial work orders in SSAM using Tricentis Tosca to ensure the map coordinates remain accurate across mobile updates.

entry.title: Re: SAC System Checklist for MySQL5 Password Change
entry.content:
Hi @ejpark, since this is a production change, the "Integration Seams" between your Cloud Connector and SAC Connection are the most sensitive areas.

You must update the password in two places: first in the SAP Cloud Connector (under Resources) and then within the SAC Connection settings. If you have scheduled Import Jobs, they will fail immediately upon the MySQL change, so be prepared to trigger a manual "Refresh" to validate the new credentials.

To maintain a Clean Core and avoid downtime, I recommend using SAP Cloud ALM to track this configuration change and validating the connectivity with Tricentis Tosca if you have automated data integrity tests in place. Good luck with the cutover!

are you using the SAP Analytics Cloud Agent for this connection, or are you connecting via a direct Cloud-to-Cloud tunnel?

entry.title: Re: Custom Business Configurator . Entries shows as Draft...
entry.content:
Hi @Aravindkurup, this usually happens when the Draft Administrative Data is not being cleared after the "Activate" action in your RAP business object.

Even though the data hits your Z-table, the UI keeps the draft state if the Total ETag or the Prepare action hasn't successfully signaled the end of the draft lifecycle. Check your Behavior Definition (BDEF) to ensure the draft determine action Prepare is correctly implemented and that your draft table is properly linked.

To maintain a Clean Core, I'd recommend using SAP Cloud ALM to track these configuration lifecycle issues and validating the UI state transitions with Tricentis Tosca to ensure the "Draft" indicator clears correctly across different environment updates.

To help you narrow this down, does the "Draft" indicator disappear if you manually refresh the browser, or does it stay there permanently?

entry.title: Re: Standard HANA business content using schema - SAP_FRA...
entry.content:
This is a compelling look at the 'integration seams' between real-time sensor data and core ERP logic!

In industrial environments—particularly in Smart Ports—the challenge isn't just collecting IoT data, but ensuring it acts as a 'single source of truth' within the SAP S/4HANA ecosystem. Using a decentralized ledger like Hyperledger to ground this data before it hits the Maintenance (PM) or Transportation Management (TM) modules is a robust way to maintain a Clean Core while enabling true predictive failure analysis.

I’m currently exploring how this convergence reduces 'blind spots' in logistics execution. I’d be interested to hear your thoughts on how SAP BTP can further orchestrate these high-frequency data streams without compromising system stability.

For long-term governance, I recommend documenting these architectural shifts in SAP Cloud ALM and validating the reliability of the data triggers using Tricentis Tosca.

entry.title: Re: PO Archive
entry.content:
Hi @youssef_hassnien, based on your screenshots, you are at the final hurdle! TheDelete step will only show files if the Write job finished successfully without errors.

A common 'blind spot' in MM_EKKO archiving is the residence time setting in transaction OLME—if the documents haven't reached this age, the Preprocessing job will ignore them.

To maintain a Clean Core, I recommend using SAP Cloud ALM to monitor your archiving health and ensuring that your 'integration seams' with external storage are validated. You can also use Tricentis Tosca to automate the verification that archived POs are still accessible via the Archive Information System (SARI) after the deletion is complete.

entry.title: Re: Restriction on Batch Determination Based on Manufactu...
entry.content:
Hi @yasovandanroop_talluri, this is a common gap because standard Batch Determination often prioritizes the SLED over the Manufacturing Date (HSDAT).

To fix this without custom code, you should update your Selection Criteria in the Batch Search Strategy (Transaction VCH1 for SD or MBC1 for MM). By using the standard characteristic LOBM_HSDAT and setting a selection rule where the value must be less than or equal to the current date, the system will automatically filter out those "future" batches.

For long-term audit compliance and to maintain a Clean Core, I recommend documenting this logic in SAP Cloud ALM and using Tricentis Tosca to automate a 'negative test'—confirming that a future-dated batch cannot be picked even if manually entered. This ensures your 'integration seams' between Production and SD remain logically sound.

One follow-up question: are these future-dated batches being created manually, or are they coming from Advanced Planning (e.g., PP/DS) where the production date is estimated?

entry.title: Beyond the Basics: Top 10 Best Practices for Advanced Carbon Accounting in 2026
entry.content:
Top 10 Best Practices for Carbon & Environmental Accounting

  1. Transition from "Estimated" to "Actual" Data: Move away from spend-based averages. Use primary activity data (e.g., actual material weights and energy consumption) directly from your ERP to ensure precision.

  2. Adopt the "Green Ledger" Mindset: Treat carbon like cash. Integrate CO2e into your financial chart of accounts to ensure double-entry rigor and auditability.

  3. Ensure 95% Scope 3 Coverage: In line with 2026 GHG Protocol updates, ensure you aren't just reporting the "easy" categories. Identify and fill data gaps in Category 1 (Purchased Goods) and Category 11 (Use of Sold Products).

  4. Avoid the "Carbon Tunnel Vision": Implement Multi-Impact assessments. Monitor water scarcity, land use, and acidification alongside CO2 to prevent "burden shifting" during product optimization.

  5. Automate ERP Integration: Eliminate manual spreadsheets. Use SAP Sustainability Footprint Management to automatically link emission factors to your material masters and production orders.

  6. Direct Supplier Data Exchange: Stop guessing your Scope 3. Use the SAP Sustainability Data Exchange to receive actual Product Carbon Footprint (PCF) data from suppliers via standardized protocols.

  7. Granular Scope 2 Reporting: For electricity, move toward matching consumption with renewable energy certificates on an hourly and regional basis to reflect true grid impact.

  8. AI-Powered Factor Mapping: Leverage AI to map thousands of ERP material lines to scientific emission databases (like ecoinvent), reducing manual effort and human error.

  9. Predictive Footprinting: Don’t just report the past. Use "What-if" simulations to see how changing a raw material or a logistics route affects the final product footprint before the decision is made.

  10. Establish a Digital Audit Trail: Ensure every gram of CO2 can be traced back to its source. A "Single Source of Truth" in the SAP Sustainability Control Tower is essential for CSRD compliance.

#SAP #Sustainability #CarbonAccounting #ESG #GreenLedger #DigitalTransformation #LearningJourney

entry.title: SAP BTP CF CAPI: replacement for password grant (OAuth 2.1 / RFC 9700)
entry.content:
I'm building an internal governance integration on SAP BTP (euXX) that **orchestrates org and space membership** in our Cloud Foundry subaccounts. The integration runs as SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) iFlows: incoming trigger from IAM, the iFlow validates and calls the Cloud Foundry v3 API to assign or revoke roles.

**Scope of this question**: only managing **organization and space members** — i.e. `POST /v3/roles`, `DELETE /v3/roles/{guid}`, and the related `GET /v3/roles`, `/v3/users`, `/v3/organizations`, `/v3/spaces` calls needed to resolve names to GUIDs.  

Calling `/v3/*` requires a Bearer token issued by the **platform CF UAA** (`https://login.cf.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token`), not by a tenant XSUAA — confirmed multiple times on this Community (e.g. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-access-cloud-foundry-controller-api/qaq-p/12144361).

Constraints I have to live with:

  • `grant_type=password` (which I'm using now to connect to CF) is out (deprecated by OAuth 2.1 / RFC 9700)
  • It seems I don't have direct `uaac` admin access to the platform UAA https://docs.cloudfoundry.org/api/uaa/version/78.13.0/index.html , so I can't just `uaac client add my-cpi-client --scope cloud_controller.write`.  The user I use has the global admin and orgManager of the subaccount.

Is there a way to give access to CF v3 APIs via client credent?

I also tried using the CF CLI this way on the XSUAA client, but it returns that my user is unauthorized.

cf8 set-org-role "clientID-XSUAA" ORGNAME "OrgManager" --client

References already consulted:

  • CF v3 API reference — https://v3-apidocs.cloudfoundry.org/
  • CF UAA API reference — https://docs.cloudfoundry.org/api/uaa/
  • BTP docs — *About Roles in the Cloud Foundry Environment*
  • RFC 9700 (OAuth 2.0 Security BCP)  https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1 
  • https://oauth.net/2/grant-types/password/#:~:text=The%20Password%20grant%20type%20is,be%20used%20at%20all%20anymore

Thank you in advance,

Giulio

entry.title: Intercompany Projects AR Invoice - Itemization required
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We have implemented 4AN and 16T as a part of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Professional Services . As a part of the Intercompany Project Process - resource related billing we have created Intercompany sales order created. When we generate an Intercompany AR invoice it only contains an itemized list of all Roles [staffing Roles] shared across Intercompany Customer Projects and the corresponding amount calculated corresponding to time booked and  the ICO cost rates maintained for the same.
In case the same role is shared across projects the amount corresponding to different projects are consolidated into one single item.

The requirement is to have an end to end traceability :
1. Time and Amount granularity at the Intercompany Invoice level corresponding to each project and roles contained within.
2. In case an external contractor is hired on the service provider entity and supplied to the customer facing entity [which has the customer project created : Intercompany scenario] Can we link the PO on the service provider entity , the supplier invoice and the End customer Project  on the Customer facing Entity?

We tried looking for CDS views as well however couldn't find any. Would appreciate your guidance around this.

entry.title: Requirement for Complete Delivery per Sales Order for a particular customer
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have a requirement to ensure that deliveries to the customer are completed in a single shipment.

For example, if a sales order contains two line items with quantities of 5 and 6, the customer does not want partial deliveries. Instead, a single delivery should be created only when both line items (5 and 6 quantities) can be fulfilled together.

I am aware of the “Complete Delivery” indicator in the customer master, and I have already enabled it. However, this setting appears to control completion at the line item level only. Currently, the system still allows the creation of separate deliveries for individual line items, even though it prevents reducing the requested quantity within a line item.

Could you please advise how we can enforce complete delivery at the entire sales order level, so that delivery creation is only possible when all line items are ready?

Thanks in advance for your guidance.

Best regards,
Aravind

entry.title: From Concept to Practice: Inside My SAP Business Data Cloud Session for Data Teams (BDC2853)
entry.content:
Hi everyone—Kara here 👋

SAPPHIRE is just a few days away, which means a lot of great conversations about data, AI, and how the two actually come together in the real world (not just on slides).

A bit about me: I spend my time at SAP working on all things data and AI - specifically how to turn all the data customers already have into something that actually drives decisions, powers AI, and (ideally) makes life a little easier. A big part of that lately has been helping teams think through what a solid data foundation really looks like when AI is no longer “experimental” – it’s expected.

I am excited to share a bit about my session: SAP Business Data Cloud: Deep dive on business capabilities | BDC2853

If you’ve heard about SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) but are still wondering “okay…but what does it *actually* do for me?”—this session is for you.

We’ll walk through:

  • What’s inside BDC (without the buzzword overload)
  • How the different pieces come together across data, analytics, and AI
  • Who it’s really for – because “everyone” is not a useful answer
  • How key capabilities work in action through short demos

My goal is simple: you should walk away knowing how to connect what BDC offers to your day-to-day work and not just understanding the architecture diagram. Also, good news: you don’t have to be in Orlando to join – this session is available virtually too.

If you will be in Orlando and are already thinking about your data + AI strategy, here are a few other sessions worth checking out: 

      • The data foundation for agentic AI: Implementing a business data fabric | BDC2058V
      • The semantic universe: Dole’s data fabric strategy from farm to fork | BDC2742
      • How Baker Hughes uses SAP BDC and SAP Databricks for AI-enabled operations | PAR1039

Your turn 👇

Before SAPPHIRE kicks off, I’m curious: What’s the hardest part of getting your data ready for AI right now? Is it:

  1. Data quality and governance?
  2. Integration across systems?
  3. Rebuilding business context after replication?
  4. Or something else entirely?

Drop it in the comments—I’d love to hear what’s top of mind (and what’s getting in the way).

Looking forward to seeing you—whether in Orlando or online!

entry.title: Re: Is this a sign SAP is losing the AI race?
entry.content:
@JRAS thanks! It’s actually ironic that with all the push for clean core, the moment external API calls start getting traction beyond the BTP space SAP seem to say “clean core … but not like this”. 

Many companies still use ABAP development because it’s honestly cheaper, but now general use agentic programming tools could bridge that gap and actually make customers go cleaner core as non ABAP costs are even lower. 

Can you use those tools and deploy them to BTP? Sure, but then you go back to proprietary stack with limited knowledge in the market and there goes the cost reduction.

entry.title: Agentic AI and the Future of Finance
entry.content:
SAP's finance applications have transformed from traditional ERP systems into intelligent, cloud-native platforms that deliver real-time insights and strategic value to modern enterprises.  The evolution reflects a fundamental transition from transactional back-office processing to strategic business enablement, driven by CFOs' demand for real-time visibility, predictive analytics, and AI driven automation.

Finance departments have benefited from AI and Automation for years.  The earliest example is rules-based systems supported by analytics.  These systems were used for credit scoring, accounting reconciliation and fraud detection.  They basically relied on “if-then” logic to enforce static parameters.

The introduction of statistical modeling and machine learning provided more intelligence by incorporating forecasting and other predictive analytic algorithms to determine the probability or propensity of an outcome.  This helped tremendously to identify what new types of behaviors could indicate fraud.

Natural language processing is the foundation for generative AI.  By connecting to Large Language Models (LLMs), this capability enables you to have a conversation with your data.  This helps to describe what is possibly making new activity fraudulent or identify root causes of anomalies in your financial analysis. Based on the outcomes, semi-autonomous agents can execute the appropriate actions. Based on the results, semi-autonomous agents can execute the appropriate actions. 

Evolution of AI and Automation

This brings us to the holy grail – human managed agent-driven autonomous enterprise.

Future Vision of AI and Automation

 All the previous stages do not disappear. The evolve or continue but will eventually be managed by the agents.   This encourages you to get started now to get the incremental benefits of the individual stages.Sample Benefits

 As you can see in the samples, financial benefit has increased over time.  One of the biggest factors impacting the value realized from AI is the use case.  It is great to have experiments in labs to familiarize your teams with the technology, but the real impact comes from applying Agentic AI to core enterprise processes.

Example of Inter-departmental Processes

 

We have all heard that data is the new oil.  Just like your car needs the right viscosity. AI needs the right data. Connecting your systems requires more than just a wire.  Whether it is a human, an automated process, or an agent, there must be clean, real-time data. The APIs must be understandable and support consistent business semantics from all perspectives. 

If you don’t know where you are going any road will take you there. Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A) provides the baseline of where your company is now and the guidance to align teams for success.

Analysis of current processes will determine if they are aligned to the objectives identified in the financial plan. Automation of the optimized processes will enhance efficiencies.  Embedded AI and custom agents will take automated processes to the next level.

One of the key components of effective AI is business context.  Having the proper semantics for agents and skills to communicate effectively is paramount to success.

And last but certainly not least is AI Governance. Robust AI Governance provides the visibility and control of process execution. 

Platform Capabilities for your Autonomous Journey

 

Integration SuiteFinancial Planning & AnalysisProcess MiningJoule StudioCustom AIBusiness Data CloudAI Governance

To learn more please visit the links or come hear Accenture speak at Sapphire about their journey to the intelligent enterprise.

Sapphire Orlando 2026

entry.title: Extensibility options for SAP Real Estate in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello.

I am currently working with SAP Real Estate in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and analyzing the available extensibility options, especially for scenarios that have an impact on Financial Accounting (FI).

 
After reviewing the released Business Contexts in the system, and also checking the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, I noticed the following:
  • There is no Business Context available for SAP Real Estate objects to be used as Usable Objects, which limits their reuse in custom developments.
  • The number of released enhancement options for SAP Real Estate is quite limited, especially for scenarios affecting accounting behavior.
  • Based on what is available in the system, the enhancement options / BAdIs I can see are, for example:
    • BADI_RECE_VALUATION_PARAMETER (valuation parameter settings)
    • BADI_RECN_CONTRACT_CHECK_ALL
    • BADI_RECN_CONTRACT_SUBSTITUTE
    • REGO_CFD_RO_RAP_FEATURES (visibility control for Real Estate Rentable Object)

Given this situation, I would like to better understand SAP’s intended extensibility strategy for Real Estate in the public cloud:

  • What are the recommended extensibility approaches for extending Real Estate processes that have an impact on FI?
  • Are there any released APIs, BAdIs, or events specifically intended for SAP Real Estate extensibility in the public cloud, beyond the limited set currently available in the system?
  • Is there any roadmap or best-practice documentation explaining how SAP expects customer-specific accounting logic to be implemented for Real Estate scenarios in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
  • Is the absence of a Business Context for exposing SAP Real Estate objects as Usable Objects intentional, and is there any plan to provide such a Business Context in the future?

Any clarification or guidance would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP - CI - Immutable Routing Principle: in SAP CPI with Value Mapping metadata ODCP in pract
entry.content:
@Ryan-Crosby 

With my solution once you stabilish the key combinations of the header from the IDOC for the value mapping and adjust the groovy script code accordenly, the Iflow main routing because imutable. What grows is the register in the VM, for use this must have the methodology of SDIA and ODCP preferable the Domain-Centric Semantic Contract over all artfacts, in case not, adaptation also is need for each company rules.

I just adjust the codes for my current needs and thats it, the KEY contains 4 elements from the IDOC header, now the iflow is unchandable.

The important is never change the Iflow again, and also I recommend each package contains the local routing for internal flow from that package the same apply for value mappings for each package. I understand that this grows the numbers but keep all organize without mess that I saw around.

Important also that the main routing iflow, should have different for IDOC, SOAP/REST, XI etc... not all in one.

entry.title: From RAP to Mobile: Reusing OData V4 Services with SAP Mobile Services and MDK
entry.content:
When building applications with the RESTful ABAP Programming Model (RAP), the same business logic often needs to serve different consumers: desktop users via Fiori apps and mobile users via lightweight applications.

In this post, we demonstrate how to reuse a single RAP Business Object and expose it as two optimized OData V4 services:

  • A draft-enabled service for Fiori Elements (desktop)
  • A lightweight, non-draft service for mobile apps using SAP Mobile Services and MDK

This approach allows you to keep one consistent backend while tailoring the API to the needs of each consumer.

Step 1 – Create or Reuse a RAP Business Object

Start by creating a RAP Business Object or reusing an existing one.

In this example, we used the OData UI Service from Scratch Generator to generate a complete RAP application from an empty package. This includes:

  • CDS views
  • Behavior definitions
  • Service definition and binding
  • Fiori Elements UI

Step 2 – Create a Mobile-Specific Projection (Without Draft)

For mobile scenarios, draft handling is often unnecessary and adds complexity.

Instead of modifying the existing BO, create a separate projection layer:

  • ZC_EVENT_M (mobile projection)
  • Based on the same root entity
  • Without draft behavior

Example behavior definition:

projection;
strict (2);

define behavior for ZC_EVENT_M alias Event
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;
}

This provides a clean CRUD API without draft semantics. This avoids unnecessary draft handling in mobile scenarios and simplifies the API consumption in MDK.

Step 3 – Expose a Dedicated Mobile Service

We created a separate service for mobile consumption:

  • Service Definition: ZUI_EVENT_MOBILE
  • Service Binding: ZUI_EVENT_MOBILE_O4

define service ZUI_EVENT_MOBILE {
  expose ZC_EVENT_M as Event;
}

This ensures:

  • No draft endpoints
  • Lightweight API for mobile apps

Step 4 – Add UI Annotations for Mobile Preview

Without dedicated UI annotations, the MDK wizard may not generate the expected columns – either showing too many fields or missing the intended selection and order.

Reason:

  • UI annotations were only defined for the Fiori projection (ZC_EVENT000)
  • The mobile service exposes ZC_EVENT_M

Solution:

  • Create a separate Metadata Extension for ZC_EVENT_M

Example:

annotate view ZC_EVENT_M with
{
  @ui.lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
  EventID;

  @ui.lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
  EventName;

  @ui.lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
  DateFrom;

@ui.lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
  DateTo;
}

After this step, the mobile preview correctly displays the list report.

 

Step 5 – Backend Communication Setup

On the backend, create the communication scenario and communication arrangement to expose your OData service.

This step provides the service URL, which will be consumed from SAP BTP.

Step 6 – Create the SAP BTP Destination

Next, create a destination in SAP BTP pointing to your RAP OData service.

Configure the destination according to your authentication setup (e.g. OAuth 2.0 / token exchange).

This acts as the connection layer between RAP and Mobile Services.

Step 7 – Create the Mobile Services Application

Create a new Mobile Services application connected to your destination.

Before proceeding further, test your OData service here.

This is a crucial checkpoint to ensure:

  • the destination works
  • authentication is correct
  • the service is reachable
  • metadata loads successfully

Testing here early avoids debugging issues later in MDK.

Step 8 – Build the MDK App in BAS

In SAP Business Application Studio, create a new project:

New Project from Template → MDK

When selecting the template type, choose CRUD.

This is important because:

  • the CRUD template enables create, update, and delete operations
  • otherwise, the app may behave as read-only

From here, you can:

  • bind the app to your OData service
  • define pages and navigation
  • implement mobile-specific UI logic

Architecture Overview

Fiori App Preview:

 

Mobile App:

Lessons Learned

The most challenging part was getting the OData service to work smoothly within the MDK application. Additional OData vocabulary references generated by the RAP system caused issues in the MDK app.

These references had to be ignored in the CDS configuration, which took some time to identify.

Key Takeaways

  • RAP allows you to reuse one BO for multiple channels
  • Draft is useful for Fiori, but often not required for mobile
  • A separate projection + service is a clean design for mobile scenarios
  • UI annotations must match the exposed entity
  • Always test your service in Mobile Services before MDK development
  • Use the MDK CRUD template to enable full interaction

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - What’s New in Moving from Standalone to Embedded Deployment [V3.0]
entry.content:
SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – What’s New in the 2026 Update of Moving from Standalone to Embedded Deployment

As you may already know, SAP continues to recommend deploying SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA using an embedded SAP Fiori Front-End Server.

Over the years, many customers who initially started with a standalone FES have been transitioning to embedded deployment to reduce complexity, optimise operations, and align with SAP’s long term architecture.
Looking ahead, this direction becomes even more explicit. As outlined in the SAP Fiori Deployment Options and System Landscape Recommendations  (as of Guide’s Version 10.3, February 2026) under the section Outlook: Fiori Deployment, the guide explicitly states:

SAP Fiori Deployment Options and System Landscape Recommendations, Section 'Outlook: Fiori Deployment''

“Please be aware that as of SAP S/4HANA 2027 the embedded deployment option will be the only available deployment option”. This effectively makes embedded deployment not just the recommended approach, but the standard going forward for SAP Fiori in SAP S/4HANA landscapes.

In this context, we’re excited to share the updated “Moving from Standalone to Embedded Deployment in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA”  guide, based on SAP S/4HANA 2025, which becomes even more relevant for customers planning or progressing through this transition.

The guide was first published in October 2019 (version 1.0). It has since evolved through multiple updates, including version 2.1 in December 2024, which focused on SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 2023. The most recent version, 3.0 released in March 2026, is based on SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 2025.

Moving from Standalone to Embedded Deployment in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA (V3.0)

 This latest update (Version 3.0, March 2026) has been driven with strong input from SAP product experts, including Peter Persiel (peterpersiel) , whose contributions have helped shape the guide based on real-world customer experiences and evolving SAP Fiori architecture.

So what actually changed?

This blog highlights the latest updates and key enhancements introduced in the 2026 version of the guide (Version 3.0 March 2026), helping you quickly understand what’s new, what’s improved, and what to consider for your projects going forward.

Why this update matters?

Version 3.0 of this guide is an important refresh. It reflects:

  • Alignment with SAP S/4HANA 2025 and SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 2025, and important announcements planned in the future releases of SAP S/4HANA.
  • The continued evolution of SAP Fiori architecture and activation tooling, especially in embedded deployments
  • Lessons learned from more recent customer implementations, translating real project experience into more practical guidance
  • Increased focus on clean core, maintainability, and lifecycle management

Why do you need this guide?

This guide is essential for SAP S/4HANA customers currently running or considering a hub/standalone SAP Fiori Front-End Server deployment, and who are planning or preparing for a transition from a hub/standalone SAP Fiori Front-End Server deployment to an embedded deployment.

It provides clear, structured guidance to support a smooth transition, covering both technical and practical aspects of the move. Whether you are upgrading your SAP S/4HANA system or proactively aligning with SAP’s long term architectural direction, this guide helps you plan and execute the transition with confidence.

From planning, installation, and configuration to post migration validation and optimisation, it brings together everything you need to successfully move to an embedded SAP Fiori deployment.

It helps you:

  • Understand the benefits of moving to embedded FES deployment
  • Know the right time to make the shift
  • Navigate technical prerequisites, sizing, and system considerations
  • Simplify the migration process with actionable steps, tools, and examples
  • Ensure a seamless experience for users by resolving common issues
  • Minimise disruptions by retaining user personalisation and role assignments
  • Gain tips on how to enhance usability and performance

By following this guide, you can align with SAP best practices and unlock the full potential of SAP Fiori embedded deployment.

What's in it / What's new?

  • Simplified architecture in SAP S/4HANA 2025
    Some Front-end components are now part of the core platform, reducing system complexity
  • More practical, end-to-end guidance
    Covers not just migration steps, but also planning, validation, and long term operations
  • Better control of custom content migration
    Helps define what custom content to move and adds validation after migration instead of blindly transporting everything
  • Improved transport strategy guidance
    Real world options for handling transports depending on your landscape and ongoing development activities
  • Extended launchpad configuration coverage
    Includes transport of launchpad parameters, plug ins, and configuration settings beyond just apps and catalogs
  • Built-in validation step
    Dedicated step to validate custom content in the embedded system after migration
  • Enhanced personalisation migration
    Clear guidance using tools like /UIF/MIGRATE_FES_PERSO to retain user settings, homepages, and UI changes
  • Explicit system alias and RFC adjustments
    Clear steps to adjust system aliases and RFC destinations post migration
  • Smarter SAP Notes handling
    Explicit step to identify and implement missing SAP Notes after migration, avoiding common post go live issues
  • Stronger focus on business roles
    Additional step to review and optimise business roles after migration in line with modern Fiori design

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Other helpful links:

  • SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Foundational SAP Notes for SAP S/4HANA 2025
  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates 
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.
